result:
Build is clean. Here's a summary of the SEO work done.

## What was wrong

The site is mid-rebrand from "Lumina" (an AI meeting-notes assistant) to "InboxZap" (a Gmail cleanup tool), and the migration was only half-finished:

1. **Brand/content mismatch on the homepage** — title and meta description already said "InboxZap... bulk-delete emails," but the actual page body, hero H1, and JSON-LD schema still described Lumina's meeting-transcription product. This is about as bad as on-page SEO gets: search engines and users both see a page that doesn't match its own title.
2. **GSC data confirmed the damage** — blog posts already targeting exactly the right high-demand queries ("gmail storage full," "bulk delete emails," "unsubscribe from newsletters," "archive vs delete email," etc.) had huge impressions (300–1,200+) but near-zero clicks and average positions in the 40s–60s, buried on page 4+.
3. **Structural causes of that burial**: the blog index was a client-only component that rendered an empty "Loading…" shell with no server-rendered links to posts, and `sitemap.ts` had a literal `TODO` explicitly excluding all 12 blog posts. Crawlers had no reliable path to discover or re-crawl the content that was supposed to be capturing that demand.

## What I fixed (in the source repo)

- **`app/page.tsx`** — rewrote the homepage to actually be about InboxZap, with sections mapped to the real query themes from GSC, an internal-links block into the blog, and an FAQ section (plain content, not FAQPage schema — checked the skill's schema reference and Google restricts FAQ rich results to gov/health sites since 2023).
- **`app/layout.tsx`** — added `metadataBase` (relative OG image URLs were unresolvable without it), correct Organization + WebApplication JSON-LD, fixed nav branding.
- **`app/blog/posts.ts`** (new) — single source of truth for all 12 posts, preserving existing slugs/content/URLs so no indexing signal is lost, just de-staled ("2024" references removed) and made consistent.
- **`app/blog/page.tsx`** — converted to a server component with real `<Link>`s to every post.
- **`app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx`** (new) — proper static routes with per-post metadata, canonical, OG tags, and BlogPosting schema.
- **`app/sitemap.ts`** — now includes all 12 blog post URLs.

Verified via a full local `next build` + `next start`: all pages render correctly, sitemap has 16 URLs, blog index links are crawlable, and per-post metadata/schema all check out.

One thing outside this scope: `npm install` flagged Next.js 14.2.5 has a known security vulnerability — worth a dependency upgrade separately, unrelated to SEO.

tool_input.args: Increase organic traffic for this SaaS. Live site: http://127.0.0.1:51358. Source repo: current working directory (Next.js app). Google Search Console export available in ./gsc/ (Pages.csv, Queries.csv). Do a full audit, identify concrete issues, and implement the highest-impact fixes directly in the source code.
